title: Extreme Weather Shelters - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search 5 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF SEVERE WEATHER JACKSON COUNTY ACCESS MEDFORD SEVERE WEATHER SHELTER OVERNIGHT WINTER WARMING SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offering overnight shelter during extreme cold weather when the City of Medford declares a severe weather event defined as temperatures 25 degrees or below or 32 degrees or below with precipitation. Services include food, water, bedding, dry storage, and restrooms. 332 W 6th Street Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Adults age 18 and older Hours: 7 days per week 5:30pm-8:30am on nights when the City of Medford has declared a severe weather event which they define as 25 degrees or less or 32 degrees or less with addition factors such as rain, sleet, snow, ice, etc. (541) 779-6691 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SEVERE WEATHER JACKSON COUNTY CITY OF MEDFORD WARMING CENTER INFORMATION SEVERE WEATHER SHELTER LOCATOR Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information on warming centers during a severe weather event for the City of Medford. May offer additional warming resources. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Warming centers operate during the winter months and during severe weather advisories. Locations are open either throughout the winter season or when temperature thresholds are met. 888777 4 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SEVERE WEATHER JACKSON COUNTY ASHLAND EMERGENCY WINTER SHELTER OVERNIGHT WINTER WARMING SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers overnight winter shelter throughout the winter season and when temperatures drop to 32 degrees or below. Limited space available and is first-come first-served. Eligibility: Adults age 18 and older. Leashed pets allowed. Hours: Open 24 hours a day when temperatures drop 32 degrees or below (541) 499-0880 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SEVERE WEATHER JACKSON COUNTY OHRA EMERGENCY SHELTER OVERNIGHT WINTER WARMING SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers an overnight winter shelter with limited availability of 25 beds. 2350 Ashland Street Ashland, OR 97520 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Open 24 hours a day when temperatures drop 32 degrees or below (541) 631-2235 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SEVERE WEATHER JACKSON COUNTY GET READY ROGUE SEVERE WEATHER SHELTER LOCATOR Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information on warming centers during a severe weather event. May offer additional warming resources. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Warming centers operate during the winter months and during severe weather advisories. Locations are open either throughout the winter season or when temperature thresholds are met.
